root_text.sn36.15:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi, ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn36.15:1.2: “katamā nu kho, bhante, vedanā, katamo vedanāsamudayo, katamo vedanānirodho, katamā vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā?
root_text.sn36.15:1.3: Ko vedanāya assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan”ti?
root_text.sn36.15:1.5: sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā—
root_text.sn36.15:1.9: Ayameva ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn36.15:1.11: Yaṁ vedanaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ vedanāya assādo.
root_text.sn36.15:1.12: Yaṁ vedanā aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ vedanāya ādīnavo.
root_text.sn36.15:1.13: Yo vedanāya chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ vedanāya nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn36.15:1.14: Atha kho panānanda, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ nirodho akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.15:1.15: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā niruddhā hoti …pe…
root_text.sn36.15:1.16: saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca niruddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.15:1.17: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo niruddho hoti, doso niruddho hoti, moho niruddho hoti.
root_text.sn36.15:1.18: Atha kho panānanda, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.15:1.19: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā vūpasantā hoti …pe…
root_text.sn36.15:1.20: saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca vūpasantā honti.
root_text.sn36.15:1.21: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo vūpasanto hoti, doso vūpasanto hoti, moho vūpasanto hoti.
root_text.sn36.15:1.22: Atha kho panānanda, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ paṭippassaddhi akkhātā.
root_text.sn36.15:1.23: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā paṭippassaddhā hoti …pe…
root_text.sn36.15:1.28: Saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.15:1.29: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo paṭippassaddho hoti, doso paṭippassaddho hoti, moho paṭippassaddho hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn36.15:1.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha … sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn36.15:1.2: “Sir, what is feeling? What’s the origin of feeling? What’s the cessation of feeling? What’s the practice that leads to the cessation of feeling?
translation_text.sn36.15:1.3: And what is feeling’s gratification, drawback, and escape?”
translation_text.sn36.15:1.9: The practice that leads to the cessation of feelings is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn36.15:1.10: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.11: The pleasure and happiness that arise from feeling: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.12: That feeling is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.13: Removing and giving up desire and greed for feeling: this is its escape.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.14: But I have also explained the progressive cessation of activities.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.15: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has ceased. …
translation_text.sn36.15:1.16: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.17: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.18: And I have also explained the progressive settling of activities.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.19: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has stilled. …
translation_text.sn36.15:1.20: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have stilled.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.21: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have stilled.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.22: And I have also explained the progressive tranquilizing of activities.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.23: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has been tranquilized. …
translation_text.sn36.15:1.24: For someone who has attained the dimension of infinite space, the perception of form has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.25: For someone who has attained the dimension of infinite consciousness, the perception of the dimension of infinite space has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.26: For someone who has attained the dimension of nothingness, the perception of the dimension of infinite consciousness has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.27: For someone who has attained the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, the perception of the dimension of nothingness has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.28: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.15:1.29: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have been tranquilized.”
